title: Modular Mug Rack
summary: A great compromise of form and function.
description: A great compromise of form and function. This Pottery Barn inspired hanging mug rack is part of a modular set that can be used in combination with other modular pieces (see image 2), or it can be used alone!

steps.title: Frame
steps.body: Apply a small amount of wood glue into the pre-drilled holes for the dowel rods. Fit both dowel rods into their corresponding 1/2" holes. Join both 25 1/2" and 24" 1x6" boards using wood glue and 2 inch, 16 gauge finishing nails. This will form a 25 1/2" square.

steps.title: Front Rails
steps.body:
Attach the 25 1/2" 1x2" board(s) to the face of the unit using wood glue and 2 inch 16 gauge finishing nails.
Secure your 1x6x24" that has been ripped down to 4 3/4" wide, using wood glue and 2" 16 gauge finish nails.

steps.title: Dividers
steps.body: Place the (4) dividers (1x6" board ripped down to 4 3/4" wide, and then cut into 4 3/4" boards). Secure using wood glue, and 2" 16 gauge finish nails.

steps.title: Back Support
steps.body: Attach the remaining 24" 1x2"board using wood glue and 2 inch 16 gauge finishing nails. This is an ideal place to attach the unit to the wall, or you can attach hanging hardware to the back.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Fill all nail holes with wood filler. Sand smooth, then apply desire stain and/or paint. If desired add "label hardware" and "s" hooks to the dowel rod.

steps.title: Drill Dowel Holes
steps.body: Before assembling frame pre-drill holes for dowel rods in the 1"x 6"x 25 1/2" boards. Use a 1/2" bit and drill 3/8" deep. You will need to make a mirror copy on to the remaining 1"x 6"x 25 1/2" board.
